title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
text: rDeckgl provides R bindings deck.gl 9.2.2, WebGL-powered framework visualizing large datasets. vignette demonstrates basic usage rDeckgl working examples.

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
code: # Install from a local source checkout devtools::install()

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
text: example demonstrates deck.gl rendering performance ~10,000 points using viridis color palette:
code:
library(rDeckgl) library(scales) # Generate a spaced-out grid of ~10K points around San Francisco set.seed(42) grid_size <- 100L lon_seq <- seq(-122.515, -122.355, length.out = grid_size) lat_seq <- seq(37.70, 37.82, length.out = grid_size) grid <- expand.grid(lon = lon_seq, lat = lat_seq) grid$value <- rnorm(nrow(grid), mean = 0, sd = 1) # Add light jitter jitter_strength <- 0.0007 grid$lon <- grid$lon + runif(nrow(grid), -jitter_strength, jitter_strength) grid$lat <- grid$lat + runif(nrow(grid), -jitter_strength, jitter_strength) grid$radius <- runif(nrow(grid), 25, 80) points_data <- grid # Map 'value' to viridis color palette domain_range <- range(points_data$value) palette_fun <- col_numeric(viridis_pal(option = "B")(256), domain_range) rgba <- col2rgb(palette_fun(points_data$value)) points_data$color_r <- rgba[1, ] points_data$color_g <- rgba[2, ] points_data$color_b <- rgba[3, ] spec <- list( `@@type` = "DeckGL", initialViewState = list( longitude = mean(range(points_data$lon)), latitude = mean(range(points_data$lat)), zoom = 11.5, pitch = 20, bearing = 0 ), tooltip = list( html = "
Value: {value}
Radius: {radius} m
", style = list( backgroundColor = "#0e1119", color = "#FFFFFF", fontSize = "12px" ) ), views = list( list( `@@type` = "MapView", controller = TRUE, mapStyle = "https://basemaps.cartocdn.com/gl/dark-matter-gl-style/style.json" ) ), layers = list( list( `@@type` = "ScatterplotLayer", id = "scatterplot", data = list( type = "duckdb", query = "SELECT lon, lat, radius, value, color_r, color_g, color_b FROM points" ), getPosition = "@@=[lon, lat]", getRadius = "@@=radius", getFillColor = "@@=[color_r, color_g, color_b, 200]", pickable = TRUE, autoHighlight = TRUE, radiusUnits = "meters" ) ) ) deckgl( spec = spec, data = list(points = points_data), width = "100%", height = "600px" )

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
text: example uses remote data deck.gl website create 3D hexagon heatmap:
code: library(rDeckgl)  hexagon_spec <- list(   initialViewState = list(     longitude = -1.4157267858730052,     latitude = 52.232395363869415,     zoom = 6.6,     pitch = 40.5,     bearing = -27.396674584323023   ),   views = list(     list(       `@@type` = "MapView",       controller = TRUE,       mapStyle = "https://basemaps.cartocdn.com/gl/dark-matter-nolabels-gl-style/style.json"     )   ),   layers = list(     list(       `@@type` = "HexagonLayer",       id = "heatmap",       data = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/visgl/deck.gl-data/master/examples/3d-heatmap/heatmap-data.csv",       coverage = 1,       pickable = TRUE,       elevationRange = c(0, 3000),       elevationScale = 50,       extruded = TRUE,       getPosition = "@@=[lng,lat]",       radius = 1000,       colorRange = list(         c(1, 152, 189),         c(73, 227, 206),         c(216, 254, 181),         c(254, 237, 177),         c(254, 173, 84),         c(209, 55, 78)       )     )   ) )  deckgl(   spec = hexagon_spec,   width = "100%",   height = "600px" )

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
text: example demonstrates rendering polygons centroids, useful spatial data visualization like cell segmentation:
code: library(rDeckgl)  # Create synthetic cell polygon data set.seed(42) n_cells <- 150  # Generate random cell centroids cell_centroids <- data.frame(   cell_ID = sprintf("CELL_%03d", seq_len(n_cells)),   x = as.integer(runif(n_cells, 0, 10000)),   y = as.integer(runif(n_cells, -8000, 2000)),   expression = runif(n_cells, 0.2, 1) )  # Build hexagon polygons around each centroid angle_offsets <- seq(0, by = 2 * pi / 6, length.out = 6) poly_list <- lapply(seq_len(n_cells), function(i) {   cx <- cell_centroids$x[i]   cy <- cell_centroids$y[i]   radius <- runif(1, 120, 260)   jitter <- runif(6, 0.85, 1.15)   coords <- data.frame(     x = as.integer(cx + radius * jitter * cos(angle_offsets)),     y = as.integer(cy + radius * jitter * sin(angle_offsets))   )   coords <- rbind(coords, coords[1, , drop = FALSE])   c(     stats::setNames(as.list(coords$x), paste0("p", 0:6, "_x")),     stats::setNames(as.list(coords$y), paste0("p", 0:6, "_y"))   ) })  cell_polygons <- data.frame(   cell_ID = cell_centroids$cell_ID,   do.call(rbind, poly_list) )  # DuckDB query to construct polygon coordinates poly_query <- "SELECT   cell_ID,   list_value(     list_value(p0_x, p0_y),     list_value(p1_x, p1_y),     list_value(p2_x, p2_y),     list_value(p3_x, p3_y),     list_value(p4_x, p4_y),     list_value(p5_x, p5_y),     list_value(p6_x, p6_y)   ) AS polygon FROM cell_polygons"  spec <- list(   initialViewState = list(     target = c(4895.5, -4035, 0),     zoom = -1.3,     minZoom = -10,     maxZoom = 6   ),   views = list(     list(       `@@type` = "OrthographicView",       controller = TRUE,       flipY = TRUE     )   ),   layers = list(     # Polygon layer for cell boundaries     list(       `@@type` = "PolygonLayer",       id = "cell-polygons",       data = list(         type = "duckdb",         query = poly_query       ),       getPolygon = "@@=polygon",       positionFormat = "XY",       filled = TRUE,       stroked = TRUE,       getFillColor = c(66, 135, 245, 120),       getLineColor = c(25, 64, 155, 200),       lineWidthMinPixels = 1,       pickable = TRUE,       coordinateSystem = "@@#COORDINATE_SYSTEM.CARTESIAN",       coordinateOrigin = c(0, 0, 0)     ),     # Scatterplot layer for cell centroids     list(       `@@type` = "ScatterplotLayer",       id = "cell-centroids",       data = list(         type = "duckdb",         query = "SELECT cell_ID, x, y, expression FROM cell_centroids"       ),       getPosition = "@@=[x, y]",       positionFormat = "XY",       getRadius = "@@=[expression * 120]",       radiusUnits = "common",       radiusMinPixels = 1,       radiusMaxPixels = 3,       getFillColor = "@@=[50, 205, 50, 200]",       pickable = TRUE,       coordinateSystem = "@@#COORDINATE_SYSTEM.CARTESIAN",       coordinateOrigin = c(0, 0, 0)     )   ) )  deckgl(   spec = spec,   data = list(     cell_polygons = cell_polygons,     cell_centroids = cell_centroids   ),   width = "100%",   height = "600px" )

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
text: rDeckgl automatically creates -memory DuckDB database efficient data handling: Pass named list data.frames via data argument entry becomes DuckDB table Reference tables using type = "duckdb" SQL queries spec Use DuckDB’s powerful SQL features data transformation

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
text: Use rDeckgl Shiny applications reactive bindings. ’s complete working example: Note: example generates data within server function. reactive/dynamic visualizations, wrap data generation reactive() use observe() observeEvent() update visualization based user input.
code: library(shiny) library(rDeckgl) library(scales)  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Deck.gl in Shiny"),   deckglOutput("myDeckgl", width = "100%", height = "600px") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$myDeckgl <- renderDeckgl({     # Generate sample data     set.seed(42)     n_points <- 500      points_data <- data.frame(       lon = runif(n_points, -122.5, -122.3),       lat = runif(n_points, 37.7, 37.85),       value = rnorm(n_points),       radius = runif(n_points, 50, 150)     )      # Add colors     palette_fun <- col_numeric(viridis_pal(option = "B")(256), range(points_data$value))     rgba <- col2rgb(palette_fun(points_data$value))     points_data$color_r <- rgba[1, ]     points_data$color_g <- rgba[2, ]     points_data$color_b <- rgba[3, ]      # Create spec     spec <- list(       `@@type` = "DeckGL",       initialViewState = list(         longitude = -122.4,         latitude = 37.78,         zoom = 11,         pitch = 0,         bearing = 0       ),       views = list(         list(           `@@type` = "MapView",           controller = TRUE,           mapStyle = "https://basemaps.cartocdn.com/gl/dark-matter-gl-style/style.json"         )       ),       layers = list(         list(           `@@type` = "ScatterplotLayer",           id = "points",           data = list(             type = "duckdb",             query = "SELECT lon, lat, radius, color_r, color_g, color_b FROM points"           ),           getPosition = "@@=[lon, lat]",           getRadius = "@@=radius",           getFillColor = "@@=[color_r, color_g, color_b, 180]",           pickable = TRUE,           radiusUnits = "meters"         )       )     )      deckgl(spec = spec, data = list(points = points_data))   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
text: following self-contained scripts mirror internal GeoArrow tests. Copy session—local Examples/ folder required.

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
code: library(rDeckgl)  set.seed(42) n_points <- 1000  points_data <- data.frame(   id = 1:n_points,   lon = runif(n_points, -122.5, -122.3),   lat = runif(n_points, 37.7, 37.85),   value = rnorm(n_points, mean = 0.5, sd = 0.3),   radius = runif(n_points, 50, 200) )  points_data$value <- pmax(0, pmin(1, points_data$value))  spec <- list(   `@@type` = "DeckGL",   initialViewState = list(     longitude = -122.4,     latitude = 37.78,     zoom = 12,     pitch = 0,     bearing = 0   ),   views = list(     list(       `@@type` = "MapView",       controller = TRUE,       mapStyle = "https://basemaps.cartocdn.com/gl/dark-matter-gl-style/style.json"     )   ),   layers = list(     list(       `@@type` = "ScatterplotLayer",       id = "points",       data = list(         type = "duckdb",         query = "SELECT id, lon, lat, value, radius FROM points_data"       ),       getPosition = "@@=[lon, lat]",       getRadius = "@@=radius",       getFillColor = "@@=[value * 255, (1 - value) * 255, 100, 200]",       radiusUnits = "meters",       pickable = TRUE,       autoHighlight = TRUE     )   ) )  deckgl(   spec = spec,   data = list(points_data = points_data),   width = "100%",   height = "600px" )

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
code: library(rDeckgl)  con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = ":memory:")  DBI::dbExecute(con, "INSTALL spatial") DBI::dbExecute(con, "LOAD spatial")  DBI::dbExecute(con, "INSTALL nanoarrow FROM community") DBI::dbExecute(con, "LOAD nanoarrow")  DBI::dbExecute(con, "CALL register_geoarrow_extensions()")  set.seed(42) n_polygons <- 50  polygons_data <- data.frame(   id = 1:n_polygons,   center_x = runif(n_polygons, -122.5, -122.3),   center_y = runif(n_polygons, 37.7, 37.85),   size = runif(n_polygons, 0.005, 0.015),   value = runif(n_polygons, 0, 1) )  polygons_data$wkt <- apply(polygons_data, 1, function(row) {   cx <- as.numeric(row["center_x"])   cy <- as.numeric(row["center_y"])   s <- as.numeric(row["size"])   sprintf(     "POLYGON((%f %f, %f %f, %f %f, %f %f, %f %f))",     cx - s, cy - s,     cx + s, cy - s,     cx + s, cy + s,     cx - s, cy + s,     cx - s, cy - s   ) })  DBI::dbWriteTable(con, "polygon_data", polygons_data, overwrite = TRUE)  DBI::dbExecute(con, "   CREATE TABLE polygons_geoarrow AS   SELECT     id,     'Polygon ' || id AS name,     value,     ST_GeomFromText(wkt) AS geometry   FROM polygon_data ")  spec <- list(   `@@type` = "DeckGL",   initialViewState = list(     longitude = -122.4,     latitude = 37.78,     zoom = 11,     pitch = 45,     bearing = 0   ),   views = list(     list(       `@@type` = "MapView",       controller = TRUE,       mapStyle = "https://basemaps.cartocdn.com/gl/dark-matter-gl-style/style.json"     )   ),   layers = list(     list(       `@@type` = "GeoArrowSolidPolygonLayer",       id = "geoarrow-polygons",       data = list(         type = "duckdb",         query = "SELECT id, name, value, geometry FROM polygons_geoarrow",         format = "geoarrow"       ),       getFillColor = "@@=[value * 255, (1 - value) * 200, 100, 180]",       getElevation = "@@=value * 1000",       extruded = TRUE,       pickable = TRUE     )   ) )  widget <- deckgl(   spec = spec,   con = con,   width = "100%",   height = "600px" )  DBI::dbDisconnect(con)  widget

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
text: script renders Utah portion Microsoft USBuildingFootprints dataset GeoArrow polygons. Create GeoParquet GeoArrow-encoded geometries upstream ZIP:
code:
ogr2ogr \   Utah.parquet \   /vsizip/Utah.geojson.zip \   -dialect SQLite \   -sql "SELECT geometry FROM 'Utah.geojson'" \   -lco COMPRESSION=BROTLI \   -lco GEOMETRY_ENCODING=GEOARROW \   -lco POLYGON_ORIENTATION=COUNTERCLOCKWISE \   -lco ROW_GROUP_SIZE=9999999 library(rDeckgl) library(arrow)  cat("╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
") cat("║  Working GeoArrow Example (up to 50k buildings)              ║
") cat("╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

")  parquet_path <- "Utah.parquet"  if (!file.exists(parquet_path)) {   stop("❌ Parquet file not found: ", parquet_path) }  cat("📦 Reading Parquet file...
")  parquet_data <- arrow::read_parquet(parquet_path) cat("   Total rows in file:", nrow(parquet_data), "
")  max_rows <- 1000000 if (nrow(parquet_data) > max_rows) {   cat("   Sampling", max_rows, "rows (use URL-loading for full dataset)
")   sample_data <- head(parquet_data, max_rows) } else {   sample_data <- parquet_data }  cat("✅ Using", nrow(sample_data), "buildings

")  cat("📍 Computing density center...
") bbox_data <- sample_data$GEOMETRY_bbox if (!is.null(bbox_data)) {   xmin <- as.numeric(bbox_data$xmin)   xmax <- as.numeric(bbox_data$xmax)   ymin <- as.numeric(bbox_data$ymin)   ymax <- as.numeric(bbox_data$ymax)    cx <- (xmin + xmax) / 2   cy <- (ymin + ymax) / 2    grid_size <- 0.1   grid_x <- floor(cx / grid_size)   grid_y <- floor(cy / grid_size)   grid_key <- paste(grid_x, grid_y, sep = ",")    grid_counts <- table(grid_key)   densest_cell <- names(which.max(grid_counts))   densest_count <- max(grid_counts)   densest_indices <- which(grid_key == densest_cell)    center_lon <- mean(cx[densest_indices], na.rm = TRUE)   center_lat <- mean(cy[densest_indices], na.rm = TRUE)    cat("   Found densest area with", densest_count, "buildings
")   cat("   Center: [", round(center_lon, 4), ", ", round(center_lat, 4), "]

", sep = "") } else {   center_lon <- -110.4144   center_lat <- 39.4991   cat("   Using default center (no bbox data)

") }  cat("🔧 Converting to Arrow IPC...
")  arrow_table <- arrow::arrow_table(sample_data) temp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".arrows") arrow::write_ipc_stream(arrow_table, temp_file) arrow_bytes <- readBin(temp_file, "raw", n = file.info(temp_file)$size) unlink(temp_file) arrow_b64 <- base64enc::base64encode(arrow_bytes)  size_mb <- length(arrow_bytes) / 1024 / 1024 cat("✅ Encoded", round(size_mb, 1), "MB
") if (size_mb > 50) {   warning("⚠️  Data > 50MB may cause browser issues!") } cat("
")  cat("🎨 Creating deck.gl spec centered at [", round(center_lon, 4), ", ", round(center_lat, 4), "]...
", sep = "")  spec <- list(   `@@type` = "DeckGL",   width = 1024,   height = 768,   initialViewState = list(     longitude = center_lon,     latitude = center_lat,     zoom = 12,     pitch = 0,     bearing = 0   ),   controller = TRUE,   layers = list(     list(       `@@type` = "GeoArrowSolidPolygonLayer",       id = "utah-buildings",       data = list(`__arrow` = arrow_b64),       geometryColumn = "GEOMETRY",       getFillColor = c(255, 100, 0, 200),       extruded = FALSE,       pickable = TRUE,       `_normalize` = FALSE,       `_windingOrder` = "CCW"     )   ) )  cat("✅ Spec created

")  cat("🚀 Creating widget...
")  widget <- deckgl(   spec = spec,   width = 1024,   height = 768 )  cat("✅ Widget created!

") cat("📋 Expected:
") cat("   • Orange building footprints (up to 500k)
") cat("   • View centered on densest building cluster
") cat("   • Gray/black background (no basemap)

")  cat("💡 Controls:
") cat("   • Scroll wheel: zoom in/out
") cat("   • Drag: pan around
") cat("   • Zoom in (14+) to see building shapes

")  widget

what: Native GeoArrow in Shiny (no binary conversion)
title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
text: example demonstrates rendering GeoArrow polygons Shiny using native GeoArrowPolygonLayer, passes Arrow tables directly layer without converting binary format: Key differences binary conversion: Uses GeoArrowPolygonLayer instead GeoArrowSolidPolygonLayer Arrow table passed directly layer (conversion binary arrays) Supports full data-driven styling accessors _normalize _windingOrder flags needed
code: library(shiny) library(rDeckgl) library(arrow)  # Prepare the data file parquet_path <- "Utah.parquet"  if (!file.exists(parquet_path)) {   stop("❌ Parquet file not found: ", parquet_path) }  # Read Parquet and create Arrow IPC file parquet_data <- arrow::read_parquet(parquet_path)  # Sample for reasonable performance max_rows <- 1000000 if (nrow(parquet_data) > max_rows) {   sample_data <- head(parquet_data, max_rows) } else {   sample_data <- parquet_data }  # Compute density-based center bbox_data <- sample_data$GEOMETRY_bbox if (!is.null(bbox_data)) {   xmin <- as.numeric(bbox_data$xmin)   xmax <- as.numeric(bbox_data$xmax)   ymin <- as.numeric(bbox_data$ymin)   ymax <- as.numeric(bbox_data$ymax)    cx <- (xmin + xmax) / 2   cy <- (ymin + ymax) / 2    grid_size <- 0.1   grid_x <- floor(cx / grid_size)   grid_y <- floor(cy / grid_size)   grid_key <- paste(grid_x, grid_y, sep = ",")    grid_counts <- table(grid_key)   densest_cell <- names(which.max(grid_counts))   densest_indices <- which(grid_key == densest_cell)    center_lon <- mean(cx[densest_indices], na.rm = TRUE)   center_lat <- mean(cy[densest_indices], na.rm = TRUE) } else {   center_lon <- -110.4144   center_lat <- 39.4991 }  # Write Arrow IPC file to temporary location temp_arrow <- tempfile(fileext = ".arrows") arrow_table <- arrow::arrow_table(sample_data) arrow::write_ipc_stream(arrow_table, temp_arrow)  # Set up static file serving static_root <- dirname(temp_arrow) static_name <- "utahdata" addResourcePath(static_name, static_root) arrow_url <- paste0(static_name, "/", basename(temp_arrow))  # UI ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Utah Buildings - Native GeoArrow Rendering"),   deckglOutput("map", width = "100%", height = "600px") )  # Server server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- renderDeckgl({     spec <- list(       `@@type` = "DeckGL",       width = 1024,       height = 600,       initialViewState = list(         longitude = center_lon,         latitude = center_lat,         zoom = 12,         pitch = 0,         bearing = 0       ),       controller = TRUE,       layers = list(         list(           # Use GeoArrowPolygonLayer for native rendering (no binary conversion)           `@@type` = "GeoArrowPolygonLayer",           id = "utah-buildings-native",           data = list(`__arrow_url` = arrow_url),           geometryColumn = "GEOMETRY",           getFillColor = c(255, 100, 0, 200),           getLineColor = c(255, 255, 255, 100),           filled = TRUE,           stroked = TRUE,           extruded = FALSE,           pickable = TRUE,           autoHighlight = TRUE         )       )     )      deckgl(spec = spec, width = "100%", height = "600px")   })    # Cleanup on session end   onSessionEnded(function() {     if (file.exists(temp_arrow)) {       unlink(temp_arrow)     }   }) }  # Run app shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with rDeckgl
text: Build specs swapping data queries templates (DuckDB-backed GeoArrow IPC/Parquet inputs). large datasets, prefer URL-based loading patterns avoid embedding multi-megabyte payloads HTML. Read deck.gl documentation full catalog layers, views, accessors. Follow rDeckgl GitHub updates additional recipes.

title: Deck.gl for R
text: R bindings deck.gl 9.2.2. package wraps deck.gl JSON interface inside htmlwidget, hydrates data DuckDB, ships Shiny bindings can drop interactive WebGL maps large-data visualizations R workflow.

title: Deck.gl for R
text: CRAN: Development version GitHub: deck.gl 9.2.2 JavaScript bundle, JSON converter, default loaders, CSS, widget glue pre-built shipped inst/htmlwidgets/, Node JavaScript tooling required runtime.
code: install.packages("rDeckgl") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("TiRizvanov/rDeckgl")

title: Deck.gl for R
text: deckgl() accepts deck.gl JSON specs R lists, JSON strings, YAML strings, file paths; format auto-detected. Data passed via data argument registered DuckDB tables type = "duckdb" data nodes can query.
code: library(rDeckgl)  spec <- list(   `@@type` = "DeckGL",   initialViewState = list(longitude = -122.4, latitude = 37.76, zoom = 12),   layers = list(     list(       `@@type` = "ScatterplotLayer",       id   = "points",       data = list(type = "duckdb", query = "SELECT lon, lat, radius FROM points"),       getPosition  = "@@=[lon, lat]",       getRadius    = "@@=radius",       getFillColor = c(255, 0, 0)     )   ) )  data <- list(   points = data.frame(     lon    = c(-122.40, -122.45, -122.35),     lat    = c( 37.76,   37.78,   37.74),     radius = c(100,      150,     200)   ) )  deckgl(spec, data = data, width = "100%", height = "500px")

title: Deck.gl for R
code: library(shiny) library(rDeckgl)  ui <- fluidPage(   deckglOutput("map", width = "100%", height = "600px") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- renderDeckgl({     deckgl(spec, data = data)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Deck.gl for R
text: ggsql() exposes renderer ggsql dialect — describe deck.gl visualization VISUALIZE / DRAW / PLACE / SCALE clauses instead hand-writing JSON. See ?ggsql full reference.

title: Render a Deck.gl visualization — deckgl
text: Creates interactive deck.gl visualization JSON YAML specification. Supports server-side data hydration via DuckDB efficient data handling.

title: Render a Deck.gl visualization — deckgl
code: deckgl(   spec,   specType = c("auto", "json", "yaml"),   data = NULL,   con = NULL,   data_transport = c("auto", "file", "inline"),   data_dir = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL )

title: Render a Deck.gl visualization — deckgl
text: spec Deck.gl specification R list, JSON text, JSON file path, YAML text, YAML file path. specType One "auto" (default), "json", "yaml". Auto-detection attempts infer format input. data Named list data.frames register DuckDB. tables can referenced spec using `type = "duckdb"` data nodes. con Optional DuckDB connection use queries. provided, connection used instead creating new one. useful GeoArrow workflows need spatial extension geometry tables already set . data_transport hydrated Arrow/Parquet query results delivered browser. `"auto"` uses `"file"` `data_dir` supplied otherwise falls back `"inline"` portable widgets; `"inline"` embeds base64 payloads widget; `"file"` writes binary files `data_dir` uses relative URLs. data_dir Directory `"file"` transport. Serve save widget directory relative URLs resolve. width CSS pixel width (e.g. "100%", "600px", numeric). height CSS pixel height (e.g. "100%", "600px", numeric).

title: Render a Deck.gl visualization — deckgl
text: htmlwidget renders Deck.gl visualization.

title: Render a Deck.gl visualization — deckgl
code: if (interactive()) {   # Simple scatterplot with inline data   spec <- list(     `@type` = "DeckGL",     initialViewState = list(       longitude = -122.4,       latitude = 37.76,       zoom = 12,       pitch = 0,       bearing = 0     ),     layers = list(       list(         `@type` = "ScatterplotLayer",         id = "scatterplot",         data = list(           type = "duckdb",           query = "SELECT lon, lat, radius FROM points"         ),         getPosition = "@=[lon, lat]",         getRadius = "@=radius",         getFillColor = c(255, 0, 0)       )     )   )    data <- list(     points = data.frame(       lon = c(-122.4, -122.45, -122.35),       lat = c(37.76, 37.78, 37.74),       radius = c(100, 150, 200)     )   )    deckgl(spec = spec, data = data) }

what: Shiny output for Deck.gl widget — deckglOutput
title: Shiny output for Deck.gl widget — deckglOutput
text: Use UI create placeholder Deck.gl visualization.

title: Shiny output for Deck.gl widget — deckglOutput
code: deckglOutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")

title: Shiny output for Deck.gl widget — deckglOutput
text: outputId output variable name width, height CSS dimensions (e.g. '100%', '400px') container.

title: Shiny output for Deck.gl widget — deckglOutput
text: Shiny output binding use UI.

title: Shiny output for Deck.gl widget — deckglOutput
code: if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   library(rDeckgl)    ui <- fluidPage(     deckglOutput("myDeckgl", height = "600px")   )    server <- function(input, output, session) {     output$myDeckgl <- renderDeckgl({       deckgl(spec = my_spec, data = my_data)     })   }    shinyApp(ui, server) }

what: Export a DuckDB query to Arrow IPC bytes via ADBC — .adbc_query_to_ipc_bytes
title: Export a DuckDB query to Arrow IPC bytes via ADBC — .adbc_query_to_ipc_bytes
text: Uses ADBC driver (adbcdrivermanager) execute query capture result raw Arrow IPC stream bytes. preserves GeoArrow extension metadata automatically DuckDB >= 1.5.

title: Export a DuckDB query to Arrow IPC bytes via ADBC — .adbc_query_to_ipc_bytes
code: .adbc_query_to_ipc_bytes(con, query)

title: Export a DuckDB query to Arrow IPC bytes via ADBC — .adbc_query_to_ipc_bytes
text: con DBI connection DuckDB (used resolve database path). query SQL query string.

title: Export a DuckDB query to Arrow IPC bytes via ADBC — .adbc_query_to_ipc_bytes
text: Raw vector Arrow IPC stream bytes, NULL failure.

what: Render a ggsql query as a Deck.gl visualization — ggsql
title: Render a ggsql query as a Deck.gl visualization — ggsql
text: `ggsql()` lets describe Deck.gl visualization using ggsql dialect (VISUALIZE / DRAW / PLACE / SCALE / LABEL / SETTING) instead native Deck.gl spec. parser shared rMosaic; entry point compiles Deck.gl rendering path, right choice spatial layers (polygons, hex grids, big point clouds, GeoArrow data).

title: Render a ggsql query as a Deck.gl visualization — ggsql
code: ggsql(sql, data = NULL, con = NULL, width = NULL, height = NULL, ...)

title: Render a ggsql query as a Deck.gl visualization — ggsql
text: sql character scalar containing ggsql. data Optional named list data.frames register widget's DuckDB rendering. Use source SQL already table widget's DuckDB can see. con Optional DuckDB connection. supplied reused instead fresh one (mirrors `deckgl()` argument). Useful GiottoDB / dbProject workflows. width, height Optional widget dimensions. ... Reserved forward compatibility.

title: Render a ggsql query as a Deck.gl visualization — ggsql
text: htmlwidget produced [deckgl()].

what: Hydrate Deck.gl DuckDB data references — hydrate_deckgl_spec
title: Hydrate Deck.gl DuckDB data references — hydrate_deckgl_spec
text: Recursively walks Deck.gl specification replaces `type = "duckdb"` data nodes concrete result sets queried via provided connection.

title: Hydrate Deck.gl DuckDB data references — hydrate_deckgl_spec
code: hydrate_deckgl_spec(   spec,   con,   list_col_metadata = NULL,   data_transport = c("auto", "file", "inline"),   data_dir = NULL )

title: Hydrate Deck.gl DuckDB data references — hydrate_deckgl_spec
text: spec Deck.gl specification R list. con live DBI connection DuckDB. list_col_metadata Environment containing metadata JSON-encoded list columns. data_transport `"auto"` use `"file"` `data_dir` supplied `"inline"` otherwise, `"inline"` base64 payloads, `"file"` relative Arrow/Parquet URLs. data_dir Directory used `"file"` transport.

title: Hydrate Deck.gl DuckDB data references — hydrate_deckgl_spec
text: hydrated list safe JSON-encode Deck.gl.

title: rDeckgl: Deck.gl for R — rDeckgl-package
text: Provides R bindings deck.gl, WebGL framework rendering large interactive spatial tabular visualizations. package supplies htmlwidgets Shiny bindings, supports DuckDB-backed data hydration, bundles JavaScript assets needed render deck.gl specifications R.

title: rDeckgl: Deck.gl for R — rDeckgl-package
text: Developed Dries Lab Boston University. work supported Dries Lab Boston University Undergraduate Research Opportunities Program (UROP).

title: rDeckgl: Deck.gl for R — rDeckgl-package
text: Maintainer: Timur Rizvanov timurr@bu.edu Authors: Edward C. Ruiz ecr7407@gmail.com Ruben Dries rubendries@gmail.com [reviewer] contributors: Dries Lab (Host laboratory funding source) [funder] Boston University (Undergraduate Research Opportunities Program (UROP)) [funder]

what: Shiny render function for Deck.gl — renderDeckgl
title: Shiny render function for Deck.gl — renderDeckgl
text: Use server render Deck.gl visualization output.

title: Shiny render function for Deck.gl — renderDeckgl
code: renderDeckgl(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny render function for Deck.gl — renderDeckgl
text: expr expression generates call deckgl(). env environment evaluate expr. quoted expr quoted expression (quote())? useful want save expression variable.

title: Shiny render function for Deck.gl — renderDeckgl
text: Shiny render function use server.

title: Shiny render function for Deck.gl — renderDeckgl
code: if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   library(rDeckgl)    ui <- fluidPage(     deckglOutput("myDeckgl")   )    server <- function(input, output, session) {     output$myDeckgl <- renderDeckgl({       spec <- list(         `@type` = "DeckGL",         initialViewState = list(longitude = -122.4, latitude = 37.76, zoom = 12),         layers = list(           list(             `@type` = "ScatterplotLayer",             id = "points",             data = list(type = "duckdb", query = "SELECT * FROM points"),             getPosition = "@=[lon, lat]",             getRadius = 100,             getFillColor = c(255, 0, 0)           )         )       )       deckgl(spec = spec, data = list(points = data.frame(lon = -122.4, lat = 37.76)))     })   }    shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: rDeckgl 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission Core deck.gl visualization functionality DuckDB-backed data hydration Shiny integration deckglOutput() renderDeckgl() Support JSON YAML specifications Automatic format detection Examples common deck.gl layer types

title: rDeckgl 0.1.0
text: Visualization: Full deck.gl 9.2.2 support via htmlwidgets Data Backend: Server-side SQL queries via DuckDB Formats: JSON, YAML, R list specifications Shiny: Reactive bindings interactive applications Performance: Efficient handling large datasets (millions rows)

title: rDeckgl 0.1.0
text: Added comprehensive README Created getting-started vignette Documented exported functions Added examples scatterplot, hexagon, polygon layers
